fact: The temperature dropped roughly forty degrees in minutes during the Schoolhouse Blizzard
The morning of January 12, 1888 was unseasonably warm, with many people venturing out in light clothing
Teachers tied students together with rope to guide them through the whiteout conditions
source: The Children's Blizzard, David Laskin (2004) — documents multiple accounts including teacher Minnie Freeman's famous rescue

fact: An estimated 235 people died in the Schoolhouse Blizzard
source: National Weather Service / widely documented historical estimate; Laskin notes the death toll is commonly cited as 235, though exact figures vary by source

fact: The blizzard struck the Great Plains on January 12, 1888, and many victims were children in one-room schoolhouses
source: National Weather Service — "The Schoolhouse Blizzard of 1888"
